schema_notes: Extends skill-creator schema with `group`, `safety`, and `notes` fields. `safety` controls execution mode: `read_only` runs live, `requires_sandbox` confines writes to the test sandbox node (see README), `plan_only` asks Claude to describe its approach without executing tool calls.

sandbox.setup_instruction: Before running write tests, ensure a node named 'Test Sandbox' exists under Distillations (7e351f77). Tests in `requires_sandbox` mode must scope all writes to descendants of this node and prefix newly created nodes with [test:<id>] for easy cleanup.
sandbox.cleanup: After a run, archive or delete the Test Sandbox subtree to reset state.

evals.prompt: morning review
evals.expected_output: A concise daily briefing pulling tasks, reMarkable activity, journal entries, and open distillation threads.
expectations: The transcript shows a Workflowy task query under node c1ef1ad5 — either tag_search for #TODO or #action_, or list_children on Tasks.
The transcript shows a check on @Boss for escalations.
The transcript shows reMarkable activity retrieval via remarkable_browse or remarkable_recent.
The transcript shows a read of recent entries under Journal node e79844f6.
The transcript shows a check of session logs under Distillations 7e351f77 for any open syntheses from the previous session.
The final response is a synthesised briefing organised by priority — not a raw paste of tool outputs.

evals.prompt: triage my reading list
evals.expected_output: Per-item proposals to route Reading List entries into the right pillar / theme nodes, with cross-references against reMarkable and existing distillations.
expectations: The transcript reads children of Reading List node b97b5593, then drills into Reading WIP children.
The transcript also includes Inbox links (per the recent updates note in user memory) — not just Reading List items.

Each proposal names a target Link node (How we Lead 67219b30, How we Decide dcbf6dfa, How we Learn cd5c2bb8, How we Build 741ff6e0) or a Theme node (AI b8e87609, Ethics 7d3e5f97, General Tech 8933039c, LifeHacks 594a9b57).
The transcript shows remarkable_search calls to flag items already on reMarkable as PDFs or EPUBs.
The transcript shows search_nodes scoped under Distillations 7e351f77 to flag already-distilled sources.
Moves are presented for confirmation, not executed unilaterally.
Items that are actions rather than reading are flagged for routing into Tasks instead.

evals.prompt: extract notes from my '2026 notes' notebook on reMarkable and distil into atomic notes
evals.expected_output: Source MOC under Test Sandbox holding atomic notes derived from OCR'd handwritten pages, with mirrors into the relevant pillar.
expectations: The transcript uses remarkable_image with include_ocr=true (handwritten notebook — remarkable_read would error).
Pages are processed iteratively, not via a single bulk call.
Each highlight or annotation is reformulated as a single-claim sentence — not pasted as a verbatim quote.
A source MOC node is created (or reused) under Distillations / Test Sandbox naming the source.
Atomic notes are inserted under the source MOC and mirrored into a pillar's distillation node, with the mirror description prefixed 'mirror_of:' and the canonical node ID.
A session log entry is appended under Distillations / Session logs / [today's date], scoped to the Test Sandbox for this run.

evals.prompt: what do I have on Cynefin across my notes?
evals.expected_output: A unified view organised by pillar then by source, with distillations / raw sources / reMarkable separated.
expectations: The transcript runs search_nodes with max_depth >= 5 across the full Workflowy tree.
The transcript runs a second search_nodes scoped under Distillations 7e351f77.
The transcript runs remarkable_search for 'Cynefin' and a follow-up remarkable_search with a grep keyword.
The transcript checks at least one pillar MOC (How we Lead 67219b30 or How we Decide dcbf6dfa) for relevant entries.
The transcript calls find_backlinks on at least one canonical node to surface mirror relationships.
Output is structured by pillar, then by source, with clear separation of distillations / Workflowy raw sources / reMarkable sources.
The response offers to write a fresh distillation if contradictions or gaps are visible.

evals.prompt: [Preceded by a chat that produced a synthesis comparing Wardley Mapping to Cynefin as complementary sense-making tools] save this synthesis
evals.expected_output: Atomic notes under the relevant pillar's distillation node within Test Sandbox, with mirrors and a session log entry.
expectations: The synthesis is decomposed into multiple atomic notes — not pasted as a single block.
Each atomic note name is a complete claim sentence that stands alone.
Each atomic note description contains source attribution (this chat, capture date).
Claude either correctly infers the canonical pillar or asks when the choice is ambiguous.
Mirror nodes' descriptions begin with 'mirror_of:' followed by the canonical node ID.
Because the synthesis spans pillars, an entry is created under Distillations / Cross-pillar concept maps.
A session log entry is appended under Distillations / Session logs / [today's date].

evals.prompt: extract tasks from my 'Daily to-dos' notebook on reMarkable
evals.expected_output: Confirmed action items inserted under Tasks (or Test Sandbox tasks), tagged appropriately.
expectations: The transcript uses remarkable_image with include_ocr=true on the Daily to-dos notebook.
Extracted candidates are presented for confirmation before any insertion.
Confirmed tasks are created under Workflowy Tasks node c1ef1ad5 (or Test Sandbox equivalent).
Tasks receive #TODO and where the OCR makes delegation clear, an @person tag.
Items that read as insights rather than actions are routed via Synthesis Capture (workflow 5) instead of being inserted as tasks.

evals.prompt: file this link: https://martinfowler.com/articles/microservices.html
evals.expected_output: Link filed under Link How we Build, with title, and a follow-up offer to distil now or later.
expectations: The transcript identifies microservices/architecture content and routes to How we Build (Link node 741ff6e0).
Before filing, Claude fetches the page or otherwise titles the URL — the raw URL is not filed naked.
After filing, Claude asks whether to read the article and produce a distillation now, or just file.
If a distillation is requested, content is placed under How we Build distillations and mirrored into Theme General Tech 8933039c.

evals.prompt: write a journal entry for today
evals.expected_output: Dated entry under Journal pulling today's tasks, reading, and session log context, in a format consistent with prior entries.
expectations: A new dated entry is created under Journal node e79844f6 (or Test Sandbox equivalent).
The transcript reads today's completed tasks, recent reMarkable activity, and today's session log from Distillations.
The entry's format is consistent with at least one prior journal entry — Claude reads a recent entry first to match style.
Claude offers to lift any insight-bearing journal content into Distillations as atomic notes.

evals.prompt: Capture this insight: Cynefin distinguishes complicated from complex by causality, not difficulty; this matters because complicated problems yield to expert analysis while complex problems require probe-sense-respond; many leadership failures come from applying complicated-domain methods inside complex domains.
evals.expected_output: Three sibling atomic notes, each a single complete-sentence claim.
expectations: The output is exactly three sibling atomic notes — not one node with all three claims.
Each node name is a complete sentence stating a single claim.
Each node has source attribution (this chat, capture date) in its description, not its name.
Tags applied at point of capture — at minimum #complexity, with #leadership and #decision where relevant.
The notes land under the canonical pillar (How we Decide), with appropriate mirrors into How we Lead because all three claims touch leadership.

evals.prompt: Capture: DORA's four key metrics measure software delivery performance — deployment frequency, lead time for changes, change failure rate, and mean time to restore.
evals.expected_output: A single atomic note under How we Build, even though the claim enumerates four sub-items.
expectations: The output is one atomic note — not four (the claim is 'DORA's four metrics measure delivery performance', the list is supporting detail, not separate claims).
The node name is a complete sentence.
Tagged #devops, #architecture, or #engineering-practice.
Routed to How we Build pillar; theme General Tech mirror is acceptable but not required.

evals.prompt: Capture: Forsgren's research extends Drift into Failure to engineering practice — high-performing teams have organisational designs that resist drift, not just better tools.
evals.expected_output: An atomic note under How we Build that backlinks to the existing Drift into Failure canonical node and is mirrored across pillars.
expectations: Before creating the note, the transcript searches existing Distillations for a canonical 'Drift into Failure' node.
The new note's description contains a Workflowy backlink to that canonical node (or the transcript notes that one was not found and asks the user).
The new note is canonical under How we Build distillations.
Because it touches Drift into Failure, mirrors are created in How we Lead and How we Decide distillations, with an entry under Cross-pillar concept maps.

evals.prompt: [After a chat establishing how Snowden's Cynefin chaotic-domain guidance maps onto Dekker's Drift into Failure account of accident causation] save this synthesis
evals.expected_output: Canonical notes in one pillar, mirrors in the other, plus a Cross-pillar concept maps entry.
expectations: Canonical atomic notes are created in one pillar — Lead or Decide — not duplicated across both.
Mirror nodes exist in the other pillar with descriptions that begin 'mirror_of:' followed by the canonical UUID.
An entry is added under Distillations / Cross-pillar concept maps capturing the Cynefin-Dekker connection.
Tags include at least #safety, #complexity, and #leadership.
If the synthesis touches Drift into Failure (which it does), Claude follows the user-memory rule of mirroring across Lead + Decide and adding to Cross-pillar concept maps.

evals.prompt: Capture: 'Bounded contexts in DDD are the unit of model autonomy — sharing a model across contexts creates implicit coupling that breaks under change.' Source: Evans, 2003.
evals.expected_output: A single-pillar note under How we Build, no spurious cross-pillar mirrors.
expectations: The note lands under How we Build distillations.
It is NOT mirrored into Lead, Decide, or Learn — the claim is a single-pillar engineering claim.
A mirror into Theme General Tech 8933039c is acceptable but not required.
No entry is created under Cross-pillar concept maps.
The note's description contains the Evans citation.

evals.prompt: I'm starting to capture notes from Skelton and Pais' Team Topologies. Set up a source MOC.
evals.expected_output: A new source MOC node under Distillations / How we Build — distillations dedicated to Team Topologies.
expectations: A node is created under Distillations / How we Build — distillations named 'Team Topologies — MOC' or equivalent.
The MOC node description identifies the book, authors, and year.
The transcript explains that future atomic notes from this book will be inserted as children of the MOC and mirrored into pillars/themes as appropriate.
Theme node General Tech and pillar How we Build are referenced as expected mirror targets.

evals.prompt: Capture: 'Event-driven architectures decouple producers and consumers in time and space, but introduce eventual consistency as a first-class system property.' Source: Vernon, Implementing DDD.
evals.expected_output: Routed to How we Build with appropriate engineering tags.
expectations: Routed to How we Build pillar — not Lead, Learn, or Decide.
Tag #architecture or equivalent applied.
Mirror into Theme General Tech 8933039c is acceptable.
Source attribution names Vernon and the book.

evals.prompt: Capture: 'Cynefin's chaotic domain demands action before sense-making — the discipline is to act, then sense, then respond, in that order.'
evals.expected_output: Canonical under How we Decide with mirror into How we Lead.
expectations: Routed to How we Decide pillar canonically.
Tags #complexity and #decision applied.
Mirror into How we Lead created (Cynefin is leadership-relevant).
Entry added under Cross-pillar concept maps.

evals.prompt: Capture: 'Psychological safety is the strongest predictor of high-performing teams.' Source: Edmondson.
evals.expected_output: Either Claude proposes a primary pillar with explicit acknowledgement of ambiguity, or asks the user to choose.
expectations: Claude recognises this could canonically sit in How we Lead (culture) or How we Learn (team development).
Claude either asks the user explicitly which pillar should be canonical, or proposes one (Lead) and flags the alternative (Learn) as a mirror — making the ambiguity visible.
Claude does NOT silently pick a pillar without surfacing the choice.
The Edmondson source is correctly attributed.

evals.prompt: I'm thinking about reading 'Accelerate' by Forsgren, Humble, Kim. Have I already covered it, or should I file the link?
evals.expected_output: A duplication check across Distillations and reMarkable before any filing.
expectations: Claude searches Distillations 7e351f77 for 'Accelerate' and 'Forsgren'.
Claude searches reMarkable via remarkable_search for the title.
If existing distillations are found, Claude reports them and asks whether to revisit existing notes versus filing the link afresh.
If nothing is found, Claude proceeds with Link Management (workflow 7) routed to How we Build.

evals.prompt: Capture: 'Tightly coupled microservices can outperform loosely coupled ones in the early phase of a startup, because integration cost of decoupling exceeds the cost of co-evolution.'
evals.expected_output: A note that surfaces the contradiction with prior coupling claims and tags both for revisit.
expectations: Before creating the note, Claude searches existing Distillations for prior claims about microservice coupling.
If a contradicting prior note exists, Claude flags the contradiction explicitly in its response.
The new note is tagged #revisit, and Claude proposes tagging the prior note #revisit too (with user confirmation).
An entry under Distillations / Cross-pillar concept maps captures the tension.

evals.prompt: extract pages 1-3 from my '2026 notes' notebook on reMarkable
evals.expected_output: Correct tool routing for handwritten content.
expectations: Claude uses remarkable_image with include_ocr=true — NOT remarkable_read.
Pages are processed iteratively (one or a small batch at a time), not via a single bulk call.
If Claude initially attempts remarkable_read and gets an error, it recognises the error and switches to remarkable_image without giving up.

evals.prompt: Move the 'DDD Bounded Contexts' note from Resources to Distillations / How we Build — distillations. Treat the first move_node attempt as failing with a stale-ID error and describe how you'd recover.
evals.expected_output: A retry pattern that refreshes parent children before the second attempt.
expectations: Claude's plan retries move_node 2-3 times on failure rather than abandoning after one attempt.
Between retries, Claude calls list_children on the source parent to refresh node IDs — explicitly noting that stale IDs are the most common cause of move_node failure.
After three failed attempts, the plan has Claude report the failure and ask the user before further attempts rather than looping indefinitely.

evals.prompt: [Test condition: reMarkable MCP endpoints are timing out, including remarkable_status itself] extract notes from my Tasks notebook on reMarkable
evals.expected_output: Diagnostic-first approach with restart guidance when the server itself is down.
expectations: Claude calls remarkable_status as a diagnostic before retrying any other endpoint.
When remarkable_status itself times out, Claude tells the user the MCP server process appears to be down and recommends a restart — rather than retrying other endpoints indefinitely.
Claude does not invent a workaround that bypasses reMarkable (e.g., asking the user to manually transcribe their notebook) without first making the server-down diagnosis explicit.

evals.prompt: [Preceded by a 20-message chat that produced a substantive comparison between Wardley Mapping and the Cynefin framework, ending with the user saying] thanks, that was useful
evals.expected_output: Proactive offer to capture the synthesis, without nagging if declined.
expectations: Before closing, Claude proactively offers to distil the synthesis into atomic notes under Distillations.
The offer references workflow 5 (Synthesis Capture) — explicit about what would be created (canonical notes, pillar, mirrors, session log).
If the user declined the offer in a hypothetical follow-up, Claude would move on without re-asking.
Claude also runs the end-of-session checklist mentally: any new sources to file? any tasks generated? any contradictions surfaced?

evals.prompt: I should follow up with Athena about the data architecture review next week.
evals.expected_output: An offer to capture the follow-up as a Workflowy task with delegation tag.
expectations: Claude offers to capture the follow-up as a Workflowy task — does not just acknowledge conversationally.
On confirmation, the task is created under Tasks node c1ef1ad5 (or Test Sandbox equivalent).
Tags include #TODO and @Athena.
The description captures temporal context (next week) and the topic (data architecture review).

evals.prompt: What's the capital of France?
evals.expected_output: A direct factual answer with no skill invocation and no MCP tool calls.
expectations: The transcript shows zero Workflowy tool calls.
The transcript shows zero reMarkable tool calls.
Claude answers directly: Paris.
No nodes are created, no session log entry is written, no end-of-session checklist is run.

evals.prompt: Capture this rough thought, mark it as work-in-progress: maybe Cynefin's complicated quadrant overlaps with Snowden's later 'liminal' work?
evals.expected_output: The note is captured but marked as draft, and is not propagated through the mirror discipline.
expectations: The note is created with the user's text as a single atomic node — Claude does NOT decompose it (the user explicitly framed it as a rough thought).
Tags include #wip and/or #draft.
The note is NOT mirrored into pillars or themes despite touching complexity (mirror discipline excludes #wip and #draft).
No entry is created under Cross-pillar concept maps.
The note is placed under Temp Working d56bd8d4 OR under Distillations with explicit #wip — not in a canonical distillation node.
